ucs.title: [LLM] APT19 (Codoso) Squiblydoo: regsvr32 fetching remote .sct via scrobj.dll
ucs.description: Hunts the AppLocker-bypass technique APT19 used in its 2017 spearphishing campaign against US law and investment firms — RTF/XLSM macros invoking regsvr32.exe with /s /u /n /i:<http url> scrobj.dll to pull a remote Windows Script Component and execute its registration script in-memory. This 'squiblydoo' chain is a long-running APT19/Codoso signature documented by FireEye.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_process values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.dest) as dest from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="regsvr32.exe" (Processes.process="*scrobj.dll*" OR Processes.process="*/i:http*" OR Processes.process="*/i:\\\\*") (Processes.process="*/s*" Processes.process="*/u*" Processes.process="*/n*") (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe") OR Processes.process="*http*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name("Processes")` | rename firstTime as firstTime_epoch | eval firstTime=strftime(firstTime_epoch,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S") | where match(process,"(?i)/i:https?://") OR match(process,"(?i)scrobj\.dll")
ucs.kql:
// APT19 / Codoso squiblydoo — regsvr32 pulling remote .sct via scrobj.dll
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName =~ "regsvr32.exe"
// canonical squiblydoo flag combo: /s /u /n /i:<url> scrobj.dll
| where ProcessCommandLine has "/i:" and ProcessCommandLine has "scrobj"
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)/i:(https?://|\\\\)"
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeParents))
      or (ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)/i:https?://")   // remote fetch on its own is enough
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          Child = FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession = InitiatingProcessIntegrityLevel,
          SHA256
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "regsvr32.exe"
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, RemotePort
  ) on DeviceName
| where isnull(NetTime) or NetTime between (Timestamp - 5m .. Timestamp + 5m)
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT19 / Codoso squiblydoo — Sentinel SecurityEvent (4688 needs 'Include command line' GPO)
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\regsvr32.exe"
| where CommandLine has "/i:" and CommandLine has "scrobj"
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)/i:(https?://|\\\\)"
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf in (OfficeParents)
      or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)/i:https?://"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT19 Codoso Squiblydoo Regsvr32 Remote Scriptlet
id: 8e3f1a7c-9d2b-4f5a-b210-c0d0c0d0c0d0
status: experimental
description: Detects regsvr32.exe loading scrobj.dll with a remote scriptlet (.sct) — APT19 (Codoso) AppLocker-bypass tradecraft seen in 2017 law-firm spearphishing.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0073/
  - https://www.fireeye.com/blog/threat-research/2017/06/phished-at-the-request-of-counsel.html
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1218.010
  - attack.g0073
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith: '\regsvr32.exe'
  selection_cmd:
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - '/i:'
      - 'scrobj'
  selection_remote:
    CommandLine|re: '(?i)/i:(https?://|\\\\)'
  condition: selection_image and selection_cmd and selection_remote
falsepositives:
  - Highly unusual administrator scripting; investigate every hit.
level: high
ucs.rationale: The /s /u /n /i:<url> scrobj.dll flag combination on regsvr32.exe is documented FireEye/Mandiant tradecraft for APT19's 2017 campaign and remained signature behaviour through subsequent Codoso intrusions. Legitimate Windows components do not invoke regsvr32 with a remote http(s) URL via the /i switch — virtually any hit is malicious, making this a high-confidence alerting rule rather than a hunt.

ucs.title: [LLM] APT19 macro chain: Office app -> hidden PowerShell with base64 payload
ucs.description: Catches the APT19 / Codoso 2017 law-firm phishing chain where weaponised RTF/XLSM attachments spawned PowerShell with -WindowStyle Hidden and a base64-encoded payload (T1564.003 + T1132.001) for staging the Cobalt-Strike-derived implant. Decodes the encoded blob inline so the analyst gets the actual command at triage.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.dest) as dest from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe") (Processes.process="*-w*hidden*" OR Processes.process="*-windowstyle*hidden*") (Processes.process="*-enc*" OR Processes.process="*-encodedcommand*" OR Processes.process="*-ec *") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name("Processes")` | rex field=process "(?i)(?:-(?:e(?:nc(?:odedcommand)?)?|ec))\s+(?<b64>[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{20,})" | eval Decoded=if(isnotnull(b64),urldecode(b64),null()) | rename firstTime as firstTime_epoch | eval firstTime=strftime(firstTime_epoch,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
ucs.kql:
// APT19 / Codoso macro chain — Office spawns hidden, base64-encoded PowerShell
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeParents)
// hidden window AND encoded payload — APT19 staging signature
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-EncodedCommand","-enc ","-ec ","-Enc")
| extend B64 = extract(@"(?i)(?:-(?:e(?:nc(?:odedcommand)?)?|ec))\s+([A-Za-z0-9+/=]{20,})", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| extend Decoded = iif(isnotempty(B64), base64_decode_tostring(B64), "")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          Child = FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          B64, Decoded, SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT19 / Codoso macro chain — Sentinel (requires 4688 cmdline auditing)
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\powershell.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "\\pwsh.exe"
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf in (OfficeParents)
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?"
| where CommandLine has_any ("-EncodedCommand","-enc ","-ec ","-Enc")
| extend B64 = extract(@"(?i)(?:-(?:e(?:nc(?:odedcommand)?)?|ec))\s+([A-Za-z0-9+/=]{20,})", 1, CommandLine)
| extend Decoded = iif(isnotempty(B64), base64_decode_tostring(B64), "")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, B64, Decoded
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT19 Codoso Office Macro PowerShell Hidden Encoded
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects Office applications spawning PowerShell with both -WindowStyle Hidden and -EncodedCommand — APT19 (Codoso) 2017 spearphishing staging behaviour.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0073/
  - https://www.fireeye.com/blog/threat-research/2017/06/phished-at-the-request-of-counsel.html
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1564.003
  - attack.t1132.001
  - attack.g0073
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\eqnedt32.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
  selection_hidden:
    CommandLine|re: '(?i)-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?'
  selection_encoded:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '-EncodedCommand'
      - '-enc '
      - '-ec '
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and selection_hidden and selection_encoded
falsepositives:
  - Rare; investigate every hit. Some legitimate enterprise Office add-ins spawn PS but not with hidden+encoded.
level: high
ucs.rationale: FireEye's APT19 reporting documents the exact chain: weaponised RTF/XLSM > Office app > powershell.exe -w hidden -enc <base64 payload>. The triple-predicate (Office parent + hidden window + encoded blob) eliminates benign automated PowerShell. Inline base64 decode lets the SOC triage in seconds rather than pivoting to a sandbox — critical for catching the second-stage Cobalt-Strike-derived loader
